question: One component of the NIHsponsored Women's Health Initiative study was a trial in which postmenopausal women were randomly assigned either estrogen or an identicallooking placebo. The estrogen-exposed group comprised $n_1=8506$ subjects. The placebo group had $n_2=8102$. Incidents of a combined index outcome (consisting of coronary disease, stroke, pulmonary embolism, breast cancer, endometrial cancer, colorectal cancer, hip fracture, and death due to other causes) were tallied in the groups. After a mean 5.2 years of follow-up, the estrogen-exposed group had 751 incidents and the control group had 623 incidents.
answer: Therefore, cumulative incidence proportions were $\hat{p}_1=\frac{a_1}{n_1}=\frac{751}{8506}=0.08829$ and $\hat{p}_2=\frac{a_2}{n_2}=\frac{623}{8102}=0.07689$.
